title: Short Film Fest
description: Cole Valley Nights is hosting its first all-ages short film fest and they're looking for locally made movies under three minutes. Screen your work and see other San Francisco directors, actors, and artists at this bustling community event.

title: Lit Fic at the Bar—May Meeting
description:
We’ll be discussing All Fours by Miranda July 📚 An irreverently sexy, tender, hilarious, and surprising novel about a woman upending her life.

Can't wait to discuss over drinks at Double Standard!

About Lit Fic at the Bar:
If you like reading and if you like bars, this is the book club for you! We meet once a month at a bar or brewery in the East Bay (usually somewhere near public transit) and primarily read literary fiction by women and BIPOC authors. All women, non-binary individuals, and queer folks welcome!

Join our Discord for all updates, communication, and voting:

https://discord.gg/PKv2DtJgRD

title: 🌸Free Figure Drawing 🌸
description:
🌸 Free Community Figure Drawing Day 🌸
🗓 May 9th | ⏰ 1–3 PM
📍 AAACC

Come draw, slow down, and connect.

This relaxed, welcoming figure drawing session is open to all levels — whether it’s your first time or part of your regular practice. In the spirit of the season, we’re holding space for care, connection, and creativity.

Bring your mom, a friend, or come solo for the experience — all are welcome.

Expect:
• Short + long poses
• A calm, intentional atmosphere
• Tea, music, and space to settle in
• A creative community forming in real time

No pressure. No perfection. Just showing up and drawing.

✨ FREE EVENT — LIMITED SPACE
📩 RSVP REQUIRED to attend (spots may fill quickly)

🎨 Bring your materials (limited supplies available)
🌿 A gentle weekend moment to slow down and create together

title: Lavender Literati May Meetup with the author!
description: Join us for our May meetup as we read “Girls, Girls, Girls” by local author Shoshana von Blanckensee, who will join us for the evening! The novel is described as a story of survival, first love, and growing up queer in the ‘90s. We can’t wait to discuss with you and our special guest!

title: Date My Friend (30+)
description:
[SOLD OUT ONLINE] limited tickets will be available at the door!

Showcase your amazing, single (30+) friend in a 4 minute presentation!

We're so excited to announce another 30+ night of Date My Friend!

Pitch application will be open from April 19th to 30th. Check out our Instagram @datemyfriend.sf to be the first to know!

Pitch application link:
https://forms.gle/77iWPT1fMVsmAuX4A

Feeling too mysterious to be pitched? Grab a ticket & join as a spectator. You can mingle with your favorite singles after the presentations are over! All spectators must be 21+

Doors @ 7, event 8-11!

title: 3rd Degree Dating
description:
No strangers. Just introductions waiting to happen.

You didn't find this on an app.
Someone passed you this link, and that's exactly the point. 
3rd Degree Dating is a curated singles mixer built on warm introductions. Everyone in the room got here the same way you did: through someone who knows someone who knows me.
That shared chain of trust means you're already one introduction away from everyone there before you even walk in.
The night is low-key and conversation-first.  A few light prompts to get things going, then organic from there. No rotation, no buzzers, no awkward speed-dating timers. 
Just good people, good drinks, and a room actually designed for connection.

📅 Wednesday, April 23 · 7–9PM
📍 Mars Bar, San Francisco
🎟️ $15 — RSVP here, then Venmo @DanielaRosado to lock in your spot
Spots are capped at 30. RSVP by Monday, April 21.

P.S. Don't forget to forward this to a single friend worth vouching for. The more good people in the room, the better the night.

description:

title: join our 4/20 group smoke on hippie hill
description:
my fellow san franciscans, join us at 4:20pm at 4/20 to celebrate a very, very important day... the annual hippie hill smoke sesh.

take that remote day, enjoy that california sunshine, BYOW, bring some to share, meet a new friend, bond as a city.

see you there soon.

title: Sapiens: Creative Lounge
description:
Sapiens x The Faight Collective present:
Creative Lounge
Monday, April 20

Bring your own artwork and spend the evening creating alongside others in a relaxed, open studio vibe. We have some communal materials if you wanna come hang out, sketch, or jam on stage. There are instruments available, but we'd suggest bringing your own.

💸- 5 dollar suggested donation at the door  
📍 The Faight Collective / 475 Haight St, San Francisco (Fillmore & Haight)
🕖 7–8pm - arrival & setup
Get settled, start working on your project, and meet some cool people.
🕗 8pm / Kickoff & Featured Artist
🕗 10pm - gtfo

What is the Creative Lounge?
Creative Lounge is a monthly collab between sapiens and the faight collective. its a open studio-style gathering for artists, creatives, and curious people. It's not a class or a workshop. It's a shared creative space. Bring whatever you're working on, connect with others, create.

title: Climate Week Documentary Screening - Oceania
description:
https://luma.com/9jp6daj5

Questions: contact Lindsay.bea.gordon@gmail.com 

Please join us for this special screening event with Q&A of the internationally recognized documentary,

​​​OCEANIA: Journey to the Center

​"Amid the unstoppable rise of the sea, the poignant story of a mother and her son unfolds. Their intimate narrative becomes a powerful reflection on loss, resistance, and the unwavering struggle for cultural identity. What begins as an everyday family portrait grows into a dramatic testament to the fragility of a place and the strength of its people..."
— Doxumentale International Media and Documentary Festival, Berlin

​​Join us for an evening of cinematic storytelling and community-building. The film begins on an atoll in Kiribati—a nation predicted to become uninhabitable due to sea-level rise and climate change.

​​Oceania is a character-driven, intimate film created over ten years. While rooted in a specific place, it asks viewers to reflect on universal questions of survival and heritage.

​​Mix and mingle with filmmaker Natalie Zimmerman and other ocean-forward attendees at the beautiful Donkey & Goat Winery, Berkeley’s first all-natural winery and community space. Following the screening and Q&A, you are invited to stay for a post-event reception.

​​Event Schedule:

​​6:00 PM: Doors open. You are welcome to arrive early and enjoy the winery’s indoor space and outdoor patio.

​​6:30 PM – 7 PM: Mix and mingle. Explore the ocean-themed space, enter raffles, learn about our impact work, and grab a bite or a drink. Music by Rourke. 

​​7 PM: Programming & Film introduction by filmmaker Natalie Zimmerman, followed by the screening. 

​​8:45 PM: Q&A with Natalie Zimmerman and Impact Producer Lindsay Gordon.

​​Post-Q&A: You are welcome to stay, meet the team, and enjoy the space with fellow guests.

​​About Oceania’s Impact:
Your attendance directly supports our impact program. Thank you for contributing to resilience-building efforts and knowledge-sharing programs with the people of Kiribati.

​​​For more on our Impact Program, to support, or inquire to partner, please see https://www.gofundme.com/f/film-for-climate-resilience-kiribati-voices

​​​oceaniathefilm.com 75 minutes

​​​Best International Feature, (ECOador Film Festival, Ischia Film Festival), Best Female Director (Frome Climate Film Festival), Wild Women Award (Innsbruck Nature Film Festival)

title: Earth Day Celebration: Tea Tasting and Pop Up
description:
🪷 Join me for an experimental artist run Pop Up of tea, herbs, plants, and wearable art! Let’s connect over a curated tea flight and celebrate Earth at the Rose Garden.

🦋Experiences:

🌹Picnic Style Tea Tasting “Happy Hour” in response to Earth Day at the Garden
🫖🧚Tea Service:
Wednesday, April 22, 5:30pm-6:30pm 

🌱 Pop Up Shop:
🌺 Apothecary 
🌵 Potted plants and cuttings
🎨 Hand painted upcycled clothing

👩‍🎨 About Lauren and Blue Jade Teas:
Lauren Jade Szabo is an Oakland based multidisciplinary artist. Her tea curations are caffeine free, exploring herbs, flowers, mushrooms, and clay. She believes each plant and essence has a spirit that guides, and in embracing tea, you care for and diversify your garden body. Lauren loves to hydrate and induce alchemy. She created a home apothecary for her own wellness, and found tea service organically through performance art.

Szabo holds a BFA in Illustration from California College of the Arts and an MFA in Painting from San Francisco Art Institute. Her work has been exhibited internationally and locally, including the de Young Museum, Di Rosa Foundation, MarinMOCA, and SFMOMA Artists Gallery, and is in private collections in Europe, South Africa, and the United States. She is an experienced lecturer and studio art instructor both in groups and as a private tutor, specializing in art media.  

🖼️ https://www.laurenjadeszabo.com   

🌞 To register, Venmo @Lauren-Szabo-3 with note “Tea Tasting”. Please also RSVP here to receive updates and to help me best prepare for our visit. $20 suggested donation. Tips are appreciated. If there is a financial barrier, pay what you can, NOTAFLOF. Volunteer opportunities available.   

Bring a blanket to sit on and/or whatever helps you feel most comfortable at the park.

🐞Please reach out with any questions or dietary restrictions. Thank you for your eyes on this, and I look forward to sipping with you in the garden! 👁️

title: City + Sea Opening Reception
description:
Come celebrate the opening of City + Sea  with me! 

I’ve been working on this show for the last 5 months and I’m so excited to finally share it! 

Also very excited to get these paintings out of my house. They’re taking up a lot of space.

title: Make your own unique fabric lampshade
description:
Have a lamp base whose shade you've been itching to replace for ages? We can help! In this workshop, we will guide you through making your own custom lampshade using printed fabric. We will provide all the materials (PVC backing, lampshade rings, tape, and other supplies).

​We have 3 lampshade sizes in different shapes (drum/conical). Write to us if you are not sure about your lamp size.

​This workshop is designed for absolute beginners — no pleated lampshades and no sewing involved.

​If you just want to make a lampshade and don't have a lamp base, we have some affordable options that you can buy separately.

title: Strange New Worlds’ Fair
description:
Have you ever wondered what an art gallery crossed with a living space vessel would look like? Well, here’s your chance to find out!

Schedule:

1pm => Show officially commences. Get fueled up for blast of with out of this world coffee by José.

… anomalous event, anomalous event, anomalous event…

4:37 you instigate a mutiny, taking command of the ship, because someone’s got to do something!

5pm crash land… on Earth?

Refreshments and snacks for carbon-based life forms will be available for purchase.
location: 395 S Van Ness Ave San Francisco, CA

title:
sf film fest presents: 
A SAD AND BEAUTIFUL WORLD
description:
the annual san francisco international film festival is back and ciné café is ready to attend some local screenings!

in honor of arab american heritage month, the first screening on our roster is:
🎥 a sad and beautiful world (2026)
🎬 directed by cyril aris

a love story shaped by chance and conflict, two lifelong souls reconnect and navigate decades of uncertainty in lebanon as romance collides with reality.

embark on this trip to the movie theater with fellow cinema lovers; stay for the afters at balboa café, where we'll share thoughts feelings letterboxds and tea about the story.

☕️🫖🥂

remember to buy your tickets from the official sf film festival website linked above.

@sfcinecafe ♡

title: Paint the Planet
description:
This month's Jean Henry School of Art workshop series is here!
https://painttheplanet.eventbrite.com/

Join us Sunday, April 26th for a special painting workshop in honor of Earth Day - from SF Artists Mike Ritch and George Nelson.

In this workshop, attendees will imagine and create the earth through a creative lens. Your guided session will include everything - a round canvas, paint, collage materials, techniques and tools to produce and take home unique and personal piece of art.

Hosts Mike and George will provide immaculate vibes for creativity, guidance, snacks and beverages including libation, adaptogen mushroom aperitif, and NA options.

Register with this special Partiful code: 'Goplanet'
painttheplanet.eventbrite.com

title: “Bad” art swap
description:
Come to the first annual 💖😦🌈BAD ART SWAP🐟🔮😍

While I am a strong believer that there is no such thing as bad art, I do know what it feels like to have a lot of art I’ve created that I’d prefer to no longer look at. Instead of putting our tired art into the landfill, why not give it a chance for a new home??

All art forms, all artists, welcome. Just bring your creations that youd like to trade or swap with other fellow artists. 

Ceramics, paintings, jewelry, clothes, etc., bring it!

Feel free to stay and hang out to make some new artists friends and make some art. 

Invite your friends!!

title: Dasein AI Film Festival
description:
Dasein AI Film Festival

Cinema at the Edge of Human and Machine Being

What does it mean to exist in an era shaped by artificial intelligence?

The Dasein AI Film Festival is a curated screening event focused on short films that engage with questions of consciousness, identity, memory, and human–machine relations through the use of AI.

Inspired by the concept of Dasein the awareness of one’s presence and relationship to the world the festival presents works that examine how emerging technologies influence perception, authorship, and cinematic form.

We highlight filmmakers who use cinema as a space for exploration rather than spectacle. Films that reflect on how images are made, how stories are shaped, and how meaning shifts when human and algorithmic processes intersect.

The evening is structured as a shared viewing experience followed by conversation and connection among artists, jury members, and guests.

PROGRAM FORMAT

• Curated short film screenings (most of them under 5 minutes)

All selected winners receive official laurels and festival spotlight features.

www.daseinfilmfestival.com
location: 1160 Polk St FL 2 San Francisco, CA

title: Best Friends Illuminated: An Art Experience
description:
It’s the thing we reach for before we even know we're reaching. It accompanies (haunts?) our waking moments, and many of our sleeping one. It’s our trusty tool. Our worst addiction. The rectangle we just can’t quit. It’s our best friend.

April 29, Tillage is hosting something rarer than sunshine in the Sunset in August: a tech-free art experience.

Come meet Matthew Stansell, the painter behind Best Friends Illuminated, featuring people on their phones + nighttime scenes of SF.

His paintings don't scold. They linger. They ask you to pause, be present, and perhaps to notice something different. To see something new amidst a familiar scene.

When you arrive, you'll be handed a printed guide — poetry, provocations, & quiet questions to ponder as you engage with the art.

Pour a drink. Sit with the paintings. Sit with yourself. Then find a neighbor and compare notes: the conversations waiting for you here are the ones that only happen IRL. Which means (and we absolutely mean this), no phones allowed. Leave your phone at home or check it in at the door. No, we won’t strip search you if you try to sneak your phone inside, but do you really want to be that person?

This event is free and open to the neighborhood. Our little gift to San Francisco.

Space is intentionally limited so pre-registration is required.

Come be illuminated.

title: Trip Trip @ Noc Noc
description:
For our next trick, the Sounds Worth Sharing crew invite you to Trip Trip at Noc Noc. A night of swirly, wavy, shroomy tunes that will tickle the body and the mind. It’s 8pm-close, free in advance, and free at the door. 

Music by:
- Dr Whale
- Cloudy Bird (MaryBird and Partly Cloudy)
- Daddy Long Legs
- Wet Pony

Not convinced? It’s Matt’s birthday, and basically everyone else has a birthday that week! So it’s a perfect place to celebrate 😘

title: BOOF: flirty800 b2b DJ USA (DANCE + DARK ROOM)
description:
BOOF is a queer-led underground. Come to move, connect, explore, watch, participate, or just lose yourself in the room. All expressions of gender, sexuality, race, and style are welcome here. In two years, BOOF has grown into one of San Francisco’s most vital queer nightlife spaces by building something immersive, high-energy, and fully our own. 

BOOF — FRI, MAY 8
⛓️ DANCE + DARK ROOM 😈
📍 The Foundry SF — 1425 Folsom St
🕙 10P → 4A
📸 IG @boof.party

BOOF returns to The Foundry. Two dance floors and a dark room. 

⛓️‍💥 ABOUT THIS EVENT
After sustained operational excellence in underground indulgence and several quarters of spiritually dubious nightlife production, BOOF returns to The Foundry to once again optimize your evening into a high-output, low-regret sensory experience.

Leveraging two fully activated dance floors and a strategically underlit dark room environment, we invite you to participate in a vertically integrated night of sweat, sound, and poor decision-making.

In an increasingly crowded nightlife marketplace, BOOF asks: what if the function was the dysfunction? what if the dance floor dissolved and no one was watching anyway? what if we stopped pretending this was about anything other than the music—and each other?

This is a no-spectator environment.
Engagement is mandatory. Observation is discouraged.

🖤 VIBE
dark, sweaty, charged
no spectators — only participants
